bonus for items normally metallic
124
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
Item Level and Casting Level
The level of an item is equal to the highest level of any
spell used enchanting that item. This level is used for
RRs and is also added when making breakage rolls for
the item.
If the item is used to cast spells, these spells are cast
at their own level, not at the level of the item. For ex-
ample, an item that casts the 4th level Dispel Essence
```
I spell (from Dispelling Ways) will cast it at 4th level,
```
```
regardless of the item level. Augmented Casting (from
```
```
Organic Skills) may increase the effective casting
```
level. Emplaced Spell items are the exception to this
general rule, as these items permit the user of the item
to cast the spell at their own level rather than a level
determined by the item.
mateRial costs
In addition to the time required to enchant an item,
a monetary price must also be paid for the various
mystical reagents and consumables. It is usually not
```
necessary to specify these (although a GM can cer-
```
tainly specify rare reagents that themselves require
```
a quest) and so the cost can normally be defined as
```
equal to the level of the item, in silver pieces, for each
day of work. This cost does not include wages for as-
sistants, the cost of a laboratory or workshop or the
normal running costs of said laboratory. It also does
not include the cost of the item to be enchanted, which
```
can either be created by the alchemist (or apprentice)
```
or purchased.
This monetary cost can either paid up front by the al-
chemist, or can be paid on a daily basis if funds are
tight. However, if the costs have not been paid for that
particular day, or an alchemical supplier is not avail-
able, work cannot proceed for that day. Days spent
suspending or restarting alchemical work do not incur
any monetary cost.
Market Value
Not every campaign will have a magical economy with
magical items routinely available for sale, and even in
those that do, these items are often only potions and
single use items. However, if items are for sale, a stan-
dardised price can be calculated by adding together
```
the price of the item, the material cost (above), and
```
2sp per day for the alchemist’s labor. If the price of the
```
item before enchantment is less than 1sp (e.g., a piece
```
```
of paper for creating Rune Paper), it can be treated as
```
part of the enchantment cost and ignored.
For complex or powerful items, the spells to be cast
each day may exceed the capacity of the Alchemist
```
to recover PP. Assistants (or magic items) will be re-
```
quired in such cases. Powerful Alchemists who may
have the necessary capacity, if they exist at all, will
be in demand to create the most powerful items. As
a simple rule, for each full 40 PP required to create
125
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
```
the item (per day), increase the cost by 1sp per day.
```
Thus, an item taking 1-39 days and an equal number
of PP per day will sell for a price equal to the number
of work days multiplied by the sum of the item level
plus 2 sp. An item taking 40-79 days will sell for the
number of work days multiplied by the sum of the
item level plus 3 sp, etc.
An alchemist working at the base rate, with two items
underway at a time, can earn 1,000sp by producing
```
items 250 days a year (equivalent to 50 five-day
```
```
weeks). An alchemist with many more power points
```
```
(higher level and/or a power point multiplier or adder)
```
may be able to at least double this. However, the costs
of a workshop, license, guild dues, security, and other
basic needs must still be paid, so this is not pure profit.
Of course, this generalised price does not take into
account the specifics of supply and demand, nor the
rarity of the item in question. A GM should feel free
to modify the sale price of an item as desired to meet
```
the specifics of a campaign (also see Campaign Power
```
```
Level below).
```
Batch Production
It is possible to save time and money when creating
Potions, Rune Paper or Single Use items by batch
production. This creates multiple copies of the same
item with the same enchantment. The same number of
working days must be spent as for a single item, and
the standard material costs apply to the entire batch
```
(equal to the item level in sp per day). The primary
```
limitation is that the creator must have enough PP to
cast the increased number of spells per day.
In order to batch produce items, the following spells
must be cast every day:
• An appropriate Work spell must be cast on every
item in the batch.
• An appropriate Make spell or Enchanting Elixir
```
must be cast on every item in the batch (for
```
```
potions and rune paper).
```
• For Single Use items, a single appropriate Embed
```
spell must be cast for the entire batch (for each
```
```
spell to be embedded). (Embed is not required for
```
```
potions or rune paper.)
```
• Each spell to be embedded, or enchantment to be
included in the case of Psychographer potions
```
(see Potions later in this section for details), must
```
be cast once for the entire batch.
```
Example: An adept would like to create a batch of
```
five headbands of invisibility so that each of his
comrades can have one. This will require five cast-
ings each day of the level 3 “Work Mundane Leath-
er” spell and one casting of the level 15 “Embed
VII” spell. The level 6 “Invisibility II” spell must
also be cast once. This process will total 36PP per
day, compared to the 120PP if the items are created
individually. Just as during individual creation, it
will take 3+15+6 = 24 days, with a material cost of
```
360sp. For the market price, add 2sp per day (since
```
```
fewer than 40 PP are required each day). Thus,
```
the market price for the entire batch is 408sp, or
if the headbands were to be sold individually, they
```
could be priced at 81.6sp each (likely rounded up to
```
```
82sp).
```
When assessing the market cost, it is the total PP re-
quired for the entire batch which determines whether
assistants will be needed, and thus the cost.
```
Example: An Adept would like to create a batch of
```
five potions which will allow flight. This requires
five castings each day of the level 1 “Work Mun-
dane Liquid” spell and of the level 9 “Make Potion
VI” spell, as well as one casting of the level 5 “Fly
I” from the Lofty Bridge closed Essence list. It will
take 1+9+5 = 15 days to create the batch, with a
total of 55 PP to be spent each day. The material
costs total 135sp. The market price is increased to
3sp per day due to the PP requirement, resulting in
a market price of 180sp for the batch. However, this
permits the individual potions to be sold for 36sp
each.
Table 5-3c: Summary of Enchantment Time and
Cost Factors
Time
```
(Working Days)
```
One day per spell level to be cast
per day. For any melee weapon,
armor, shield, or missile weapons,
subtract any material bonus of the
```
item (but not less than half).
```
Level of Item Highest level of any spell used to
enchant the item.
Material Cost 1sp per level of item per day, plus
cost of base item.
Maximum Number
of Alchemists
One per spell to be cast per day.
Item Bonus Greater of the enchantment bonus
or the material bonus.
Base Market Value Material costs, plus 2sp per day,
plus an additional 1sp per day for
every full 40 PP required.
Casting Level of
Embedded Spells
```
Level of the embedded spell (e.g.,
```
a 1st level spell is cast at 1st level
```
even if the item is of higher level).
```
126
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
Enchanting Superior
Materials
The creation process detailed here assumes that a
mundane material is used for the enchantment. It is of
course possible to enchant an item, weapon or armor
```
crafted from a superior material (see Section 3.2). If
```
the item is to be enchanted with a magical bonus, it
does not stack with any material item bonuses it may
already have. The effective bonus is the higher of the
enchantment or material. In addition, if the enchant-
ments included any item bonus whatsoever, the item
is now considered magical when receiving its full
bonus even if the material bonus is greater than the
enchantment bonus would have been. Of course, if an
alchemist enchanted a high steel sword with various
useful abilities but no bonus to OB, a wielder would
still receive a +10 non-magical bonus from the supe-
rior material itself.
In addition, enchanting a superior material has an
additional benefit if the item is any melee weapon,
armor, shield, or missile weapon. When the number
of days required for enchantment is calculated, the
material bonus for the item is deducted from the total,
to a minimum of half the normal time requirement.
Thus if a magical sword required 43 days to enchant
```
for a given specification, and a high steel (+10) sword
```
were used as the base item, the actual time require-
ment would be 33 days. Note that while the number of
required working days is reduced, the PP requirement
is not, so the market price of a magic sword that took
33 days but 43 PP to create will have a cost equal to
the item level plus 3sp, per day.
However, the item will likely require a more advanced
Work spell based on the superior material.
```
Example: An Adept wishes to create a +10 magi-
```
cal sword which also inflicts an additional Cold
```
critical (one degree of severity less than the primary
```
```
critical). This requires daily castings of the level 5
```
“Work Lesser Metals” spell, the level 8 “Weapon
II” spell, and the level 14 “Additional Crit II” spell.
It would take 5+8+14 = 27 days with a material
```
cost of 378sp, plus the cost of the base sword (likely
```
```
10sp), which will permit a market price of 442sp.
```
```
If the Adept began with a High Steel sword (base
```
```
cost of 110sp), the level 9 “Work Mundane Metal
```
II” spell would be required, but the level 4 “Weap-
on I” spell could be substituted for “Weapon II,”
```
requiring only 17 days (9+4+14-10) for a material
```
cost of 238sp plus the sword for a total of 348sp, or
a market price of 382sp. The High Steel version is
```
stronger (less susceptible to breakage), takes less
```
time to make, and reduces the enchantment cost.
Sets and Already
-Enchanted Items
Creating an item with many powers is a complex pro-
cess. Enchantments are not merely layered one over
the other but must be interwoven at every stage. Every
spell must be cast every day, and the more complex the
final product, the longer it takes. As such, it is normally
not possible to add new enchantments to an existing
item. If an item is to be re-enchanted, each of the orig-
inal spells must be recast, over just as many days as
the original enchantment. Further, if the original Work
```
spell (if any) was only suitable for non-magical mate-
```
rials, it must be replaced with a corresponding Work
```
spell capable of preparing magical materials (e.g.,
```
Work Mundane Wood must be replaced with the higher
```
level Work Wood) since the material has been infused
```
with magic. It should be obvious that this process is
only worthwhile if the base item itself is of great value,
whether that value is material or cultural.
Re-enchantment does permit the addition of new spells
in addition to the reworking of old ones. It also permits
changing choices made in enchantment. A Slaying
spell might be altered to target orcs instead of elves,
or demons instead of champions. An additional crit-
ical might be changed from heat to cold. Depending
on one’s perspective, these changes might constitute
purifying or corrupting an item, or simply adjusting its
nature for new foes in a new era.
Three exceptions provide means to manipulate existing
magic items. One is to bind the item as part of a mul-
tiform item, so that whatever its powers, it becomes
merely one form of a more flexible creation. Multiform
items may be used to give the wielder choices or to
conceal power. For details, see Multiform Items below.
The second exception involves items with embedded
```
entities (see Embedded Entities under Artifact Intelli-
```
```
gence later in this section). The Manifest spells permit
```
powers of the embedded entity to be made accessible
through the item. This requires only casting of the
```
Manifest spell and an appropriate Work spell (bearing
```
```
in mind the item is now made of magical materials) or
```
Consecration.
The third exception is to bind the item into a Set. A
group of items each bearing their own enchantments,
normally unified by purpose, maker, theme, or at least
materials, may be crafted into a set such that additional
properties become apparent when a single individual
wields or wears all of its constituent parts. Sets can
be a simple way to add properties to an existing item
without re-enchanting it, but they also offer the risk
that pieces may be lost or even destroyed, rendering
those additional properties inaccessible.
127
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
A set may consist of items designed to work together,
such as a sword and a scabbard, or the various pieces
of armor which make up a full suit. It may also consist
of items which will be bound together, for example a
crystal which will be mounted on a staff. The magic of
the set will not prevent the separation of these items,
but it will render the collection more potent than the
individual parts. Note, however, that an item cannot be
part of two sets, nor can one set be incorporated into
another.
To create a set, the alchemist must first create or collect
the items. Their individual enchantments must already
be completed. A new enchantment is then performed,
which must include Work or Consecration spells for
each item. As the individual items are magical, any
Work spell must be suitable for working magical ma-
terials. Additional enchantment spells are added to the
Work or Consecration spells to determine the number
of required working days and material costs just as if
the set was a regular item.
```
Example: An Adept has prepared a sword which is
```
a powerful weapon and a scabbard which heals
its bearer, and now wishes to bind them into a set
which will make the bearer a more effective leader.
The sword was made from high steel and is now
magical, so the level 14 “Work Metal II” is re-
quired, and the leather scabbard will require level
17 “Work Leather.” To these spells, he adds “Gen-
eral III” to provide a +15 Leadership bonus, as
```
well as “Daily VII” (level 18), “Embed VI” (level
```
```
13), and “Sounding III” (level 6 from Physical En-
```
```
hancement) to ensure the bearer will be heard. The
```
set thus totals 14+17+18+13+6 = 68 spell levels,
so it will take 68 working days to create with a ma-
terial cost of 1224sp. Not an insignificant effort, but
still easier than re-enchanting the sword to contain
all of these powers itself.
Campaign Power Level
If the different formal campaign power levels from
RMCL are used, the level chosen will have an impact
on the production costs and sale price of magical
items. Campaigns set in a higher power level will
have cheaper and more readily available items than in
a lower power level campaign.
This adjustment of prices is easily performed using
the multipliers listed below by power level. Simply
multiply the enchantment costs and sale price by the
multiplier, aside from any cost due to the base item
itself. The GM should modify these prices further if
they wish magic items to be more or less valuable or
rare.
item tyPes
Common types of items are listed here with their re-
quired spells and other notes. Although each item type
is listed separately, a given item may contain enchant-
ments of multiple types. The alchemist must simply
cast all of the required spells for every property, ev-
ery day for the duration of the enchantment process,
based on the total number of working days for all of
the enchantments. If a Work or Consecration spell is
required for multiple properties being imbued into a
single item, it need only be included once and not once
per enchantment. Chapter 6 includes numerous exam-
ples of such complex items.
```
Armor (Adept)
```
Armor will normally be enchanted with spells from
the Adept base list Armor Enchantment.
Torso armor enchanted with an Armor spell may re-
ceive a bonus to the wearer’s DB. Any armor, includ-
ing vambraces, greaves, and helms, may be enchanted
with Armor spells to grant bonuses which offset armor
penalties to maneuvers, ranged attacks, or perception.
Shields enchanted with an Armor spell receive a bo-
nus to the wielder’s Shield skill, which will improve
the chance the shield may be used against additional
attacks beyond the first, as well as improving their
resulting DB when performing a partial or full block
```
(but not a passive block), and also adding to their OB
```
if the wielder makes a Shield bash attack.
Table 5-3d: Power Level Price Adjustments
Power Level Multiplier
Average x1.5
Superior x1
Heroic x0.75
Legendary x0.5
Epic x0.3
128
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
To create magical armor, the following spells must be
cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3).
```
• Armor or other armor enchantment spells. Multiple
```
Armor spells can be used (e.g., to grant bonus
```
```
DB and also cancel out penalties) but not to grant
```
```
the same type of bonus (e.g., you cannot use
```
```
Armor I and Armor II to grant a total of +15 DB).
```
Other common armor enchantments include Crit Ne-
```
gation (cancels some criticals hitting a specified lo-
```
```
cation which may be the torso, head, legs, or arms),
```
```
Increase Armor Type (makes armor on one location
```
```
more protective), and Defensive Size (reduces criti-
```
```
cal severity as if the wearer was a larger size). Any of
```
these enchantments may be narrowed to protect only
```
against a single critical type (e.g., Piercing), reducing
```
the spell to 1/5 normal working days and PP.
```
Example: An Adept wishes to create a mail shirt
```
```
(AT 7) which will grant +10 DB, reduce maneuver
```
penalties by 10, and have a 10% chance of negating
Puncture criticals. Each day of the enchantment,
they must cast the level 5 “Work Lesser Metals”
```
spell, the level 9 “Armor II” spell twice (once for
```
```
DB and once for the penalty reduction), and the
```
```
level 10 “Crit Negation II” spell (but reduced to
```
```
1/5th time and PP). The enchantment will take
```
```
5+(2x9)+(10/5) = 25 days, with a material cost of
```
10sp per day = 250sp and a market price of 300sp,
plus 15sp for the base mail shirt for a total of 315sp.
```
Bonus Items (Adept, Sanctifier)
```
While armor and weapons with bonuses are highly
sought-after, any manner of item may be enchanted
to grant a bonus to an associated skill specialization.
```
The General spell (but not Sacred Skill) may be dou-
```
bled to apply the bonus to all specializations of the
skill, or quadrupled to apply to all skills in the same
category. These spells may instead grant the benefit of
```
additional ranks (+2 ranks in place of each +5 bonus)
```
```
for purposes which depend on ranks (e.g., Ambush,
```
```
or passive dodging with Running skill) but this grants
```
only the rank benefit and does not increase the skill
bonus in any way.
Although bonus items may grant bonuses to melee
```
and thrown Combat Training skills (e.g., gloves which
```
```
grant a bonus to Unarmed Strikes), these bonuses are
```
not cumulative with any bonuses from weapons. At-
tacks made using such items are not treated as magical.
```
(To create an item intended to make magical attacks,
```
such as magical gloves for unarmed strikes, enchant
```
them as a weapon rather than as a bonus item.)
```
Items enchanted with General spells may instead grant
a bonus to RRs for a single Realm, Physical RRs, or
Fear RRs, and the bonus may be reduced by 5 to grant
```
a +5' radius to the effect (as described in the spell list
```
```
notes).
```
To create a Bonus item, the following spells must be
cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3b for
```
```
Adepts; Sanctifiers use Consecration, the 2nd
```
```
level spell from the Sacred Purpose base list).
```
```
• The Bonus Effect spell(s) (Adepts use General
```
```
spells from their General Enchantment base list;
```
Sanctifiers use Sacred Skill from their Divine
```
Touch base list).
```
```
Example: An Adept wishes to create an item which
```
grants some protection against fear. He decides to
craft a shield-shaped brooch to be worn over the
heart, which will grant +15 to Fear resistance rolls.
It requires a level 5 “Work Lesser Metals” spell and
a level 15 “General III” for the bonus. These spells
must each be cast every day of the enchantment
process. The brooch will take 20 days to create, with
a material cost of 15sp per day totaling 300sp and
a market price of 340sp, plus any cost of the brooch
```
(negligible unless precious metals are used).
```
```
Charged Items (Adept)
```
A Wand, Rod, Staff, Half-Bole or Bole will require
the appropriate Make spell, but this will imbue the
item with magical charges which can then be used
to power spells embedded within. Normally the user
must Attune to the item and activate a spell, although
the use of a Triggered Item spell can be linked to
embedded spells. When created, an item of this type
```
will have maximum charges (Wand 10, Rod 30, Staff
```
```
100, Half-Bole 200 and Bole 500) and a Charge spell
```
can be used to recharge items that have been deplet-
ed. Note that it is possible to create an item one size
```
smaller (e.g., a wand with the capabilities of a rod)
```
```
by using the Size Reduction spell (the 13th level spell
```
```
from the Organic Skills spell list).
```
To create a Charged item, the following spells must be
cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3b).
```
```
• An appropriate Make spell (from the Adept base
```
```
list Organic Skills, summarized on Table 5-3e).
```
```
• An appropriate Embed spell (from the Adept base
```
list Essence Embedding or Other Realm Embed-
```
ding), and also Hybrid Embedding (the 2nd level
```
```
spell from Other Realm Embedding) if the spell
```
to be embedded is from a hybrid base list.
```
• The spell(s) to be embedded.129
```
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
```
Example: An Adept decides to create a ring that
```
will once per day allow her to breathe under water.
Each day, the level 5 “Work Lesser Metals” spell,
a level 19 “Embed X” spell, a level 25 “Daily X”
spell and the level 8 Open Essence “Waterlungs”
spell must all be cast. The “Daily X” spell is re-
quired because the next lower version, “Daily VII,”
would not be able to embed a level 8 spell, and the
same applies to the “Embed” spell. Once created,
this item will allow the wearer to breathe underwa-
```
ter for 80 minutes (10 minutes per level based on an
```
```
8th level spell).The item will take 5+19+25+8 = 57
```
days to create, with a material cost of 25sp per day
totaling 1425sp. As the item will require more than
40 PP, market price adds 3sp per day. A base ring
could be of any cost, depending on the materials
and design, but assuming a simple 1sp ring is used,
the market price will be 1597sp.
Daily spells are flexible. In their basic use, they allow
a total level of spells to be cast once per day. For ex-
ample, a Daily V spell can allow a 5th level spell to
be cast once per day or a 1st level spell to be cast five
times per day or a 3rd level spell and a 2nd level spell
to each be cast once per day or any other combination
of total spell levels equal to five each day. The time
```
required (in days) for both the Daily spell and all other
```
spells involved in the creation of that same ability may
be reduced by a reduction in the frequency with which
the embedded spells may be used. In half the normal
time, the spells may be embedded to be used once per
week. In one-third of the normal time, the spells may
be embedded to be used once per fortnight. In a quar-
ter of the normal time, the spells may be embedded to
be used once per month. Making usage less frequent
may alternatively be used to embed a higher level spell
with no time reduction in the enchantment process.
This can only be done to embed a single higher level
spell up to twice the normal level as a weekly spell,
up to triple normal level as a fortnightly spell, or four
times normal level as a monthly spell. For example,
Daily V can allow a 10th level spell to be cast once a
week, a 15th level spell to be cast once a fortnight, or
a 20th level spell to be cast once a month.
```
Example: An Adept wishes to create a simple Wand
```
of Projected Light to help with dungeon delving.
Made of a non-magical wood, the wand would first
require a level 4 “Work Mundane Wood” spell. It
would also require a level 7 “Make Wand” spell to
create a wand with ten charges, and level 5 “Embed
II” to embed the desired spell. Lastly, the level 2
closed Essence spell “Projected Light” must also be
cast. These spells must each be cast every day of the
enchantment process. The wand will take 18 days to
create, with a material cost of 7sp per day totaling
```
126sp and a market price of 162sp (the cost of a
```
regular wooden stick is negligible and treated as
```
part of the material costs).
```
```
An item may be enchanted with a Weapon spell (from
```
```
the Weapon Enchantment base list) to grant a bonus to
```
embedded Directed Spells, but this doubles the time
and PP requirement for the Weapon spell.
```
Daily/Constant Items (Adept)
```
These items have one or more spells embedded within
them, which can be used once or more per day, or in
the case of Constant items, the spells is always active.
It is also possible, through the use of a Trigger spell
to create the item such that the spell is automatically
activated by circumstances.
To create a Daily/Constant item, the following spells
must be cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3b).
```
```
• An appropriate Embed spell (from the Adept base
```
list Essence Embedding or Other Realm Embed-
```
ding). Hybrid Embedding is also required if the
```
spell to be embedded is from a hybrid base list.
```
• An appropriate Daily or Constant spell (from the
```
Adept base list Essence Embedding or Other
```
Realm Embedding). The Constant spell must be
```
```
cast twice (increasing the number of required
```
```
working days) if the item will permit the user to
```
```
cast the linked spell at will (e.g., attack spells) or
```
if it will be triggered by a particular circumstance
```
(e.g., falling, inflicting a critical on a foe).
```
```
• The spell(s) to be embedded.
```
```
Table 5-3e: Charged Items (Adept only)
```
Item Size
Max
Charges
Max Spell
```
Level Make Spell (Level)
```
```
Wand 1'-2.5' (1/2" dia.) 10 2 Make Wand (7)
```
```
Rod 2.5'-5' (1" dia.) 30 5 Make Rod (10)
```
```
Staff 5'+ (1.5" dia.) 100 10 Make Staff (14)
```
```
Half-Bole 4'+ (18" dia.) 200 20 Make Half-Bole (19)
```
```
Bole 10'+ (12" dia.) 500 50 Make Bole (25)
```
Any metallic item As above As above As above As above, plus
```
Metallic Wand (6)
```
130
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
```
Example: Since the Adept’s waterbreathing ring used
```
Daily X, and Waterlungs is only 8th level, the Adept
decides to incorporate an additional “Projected
Light”, which is only 2nd level. Thus, no additional
Daily spell is needed. However, Embed is not so
```
flexible, so “Embed II” will be required as well (lev-
```
```
el 5). This raises the time requirement to 64 days,
```
the material cost to 1600sp, and the market price
to 1793sp. The ring can be used to cast Waterlungs
and Projected Light each once per day.
Emplaced Spell Items
```
(Psychographer)
```
These items contain the knowledge of how to cast a
spell. Either the power must be provided by the user
```
(in which case it must be of the correct realm unless
```
```
the Adaptive Power spell is added to the item), or
```
power must be implanted in the item for that purpose.
Emplaced Spell Items are easier for Psychographers to
create than the equivalent Daily items are for Adepts
to create, but the item’s spell is cast at the level of the
user, whereas there are various methods to boost the
effective level for a Daily item.
To create an Emplaced Spell item, the following spells
must be cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3b).
```
```
• An appropriate Emplace Spell spell (from the
```
```
Psychographer base list Power Impressions), or
```
Emplace List.
• Either the spell to be emplaced or a second casting
of Emplace spell. In the case of Emplace List,
either every spell to be emplaced or two addition-
al castings of Emplace List.
```
• Template Link (the 1st level spell from the Power
```
```
Impressions base list) if the knowledge of the
```
spell will be copied from another person and not
the alchemist themself.
```
• Optionally, an Implant Power spell (also from
```
```
Power Impressions) to store PP in the item.
```
The template individual must be within 100' of the
caster during item creation.
```
Example: A Psychographer decides to create a cloak
```
which will allow the wearer to cast Shadow. He
chooses to also put power in the item. Each day,
the level 2 “Work Mundane Cloth” spell, a level 5
“Emplace Spell III” spell, a level 2 “Shadow” spell,
a level 4 “Implant Power I” spell must all be cast.
The “Emplace Spell III” spell is required because
the next lower level version, “Emplace Spell I,” is
not sufficient for the second level “Shadow.” The
item will take 2+5+2+4 = 13 days to craft, with
a material cost of 5sp per day totaling 65sp and a
market price of 91sp.
Once created, this item will store one PP per two
ranks of Power Development possessed by the
template, and these PP will be usable to cast “Shad-
ow.” In this case, the Psychographer served as the
template, and has 10 ranks of Power Development,
so the cloak stores 5 PP, enough to cast Shadow
```
twice per day (with 1 left over). The duration of the
```
spell will depend on the level of the user of the item.
Users with Mentalism PP of their own will also be
able to expend their own PP to cast the spell, or use
the PP stored in the item.
```
Multiform Items (Adept)
```
A multiform item is capable of transforming from one
item into another, or into several different forms. To
create such an item, a separate item must be prepared
for each form as an independent magic item. If a form
will have no magic properties other than the ability to
change form, it still requires an appropriate Work spell
to be cast. The items can be created separately, or even
one at a time. Each item may have different properties,
and in fact even if it is desired for each form to have
the same abilities, each of the items to be combined
must be separately enchanted with those abilities.
In order to combine the items into a single multiform
item, the item requiring the most days of enchantment
must have the Multiform spell added to its enchant-
ments. This item must be completed last, so that it can
absorb the other items.
```
Example: An Adept wishes to make a bracelet which
```
will transform into a +15 broadsword. The bracelet
will have no magical properties of its own, in order
to appear innocuous. It will be prepared using only
the level 5 “Work Lesser Metal”, taking 5 days and
costing 25sp in materials. The sword will also be
enchanted with “Work Lesser Metal,” plus level 13
“Weapon III” and level 3 “Multiform II,” taking
21 days with a material cost of 273sp, and a market
```
price of 315sp (plus 10sp for a broadsword). The
```
final sale price will be 350sp for the enchantments,
10sp for the broadsword, and the item value of the
bracelet. If the bracelet is a 10sp silver bracelet of
average quality, the total is 370sp.
Potions
```
(Adept, Psychographer)
```
Creating potions is a very easy way to embed magi-
cal effects in a way that even the dumbest fighter can
make use of. Normally imbibed to activate the effect,
a potion is always a single use item, and can benefit
from the batch production rules.
131
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
To create a Potion, the following spells must be cast
every day:
• An appropriate Work spell. See Table 5-3f for a
summary of the required spells. Note that Psy-
chographers only work with mundane liquids
```
(using the 1st level spell from Crafts Mastery),
```
while Adepts can work with a variety of mundane
```
and magical liquids, gases, and poisons (using
```
```
spells from Fluid Skills).
```
```
• An appropriate Make spell (from the Adept base
```
```
list Fluid Skills) or Enchanting Elixir spell (from
```
```
the Psychographer base list Deep Impressions),
```
as summarized on Table 5-3g. Make Potion spells
are limited by the level of the spell to be em-
bedded, whereas Enchanting Elixir may embed
spells of any level but the duration is fixed.
```
• For Adept potions, the spell(s) to be embedded.
```
For Psychographer elixirs, the enchantment may
be any enchantment which would normally be
placed on an item in order to affect its bearer.
Enchantments which would affect an item and
```
not its bearer are not suitable (e.g., a weapon en-
```
chantment granting increased OB, or one which
```
renders an item unbreakable).
```
```
Example: To enchant a berry cordial with a potion
```
that will protect the drinker, an Adept alchemist
must, each day, cast a Work Mundane Liquid spell
```
(level 1) and a Make Potion III spell (level 3) to
```
create the potion itself. The alchemist must cast the
```
“Shield” spell (level 2) every day as well. When
```
drunk, this potion will give the drinker an invisible
shield granting +25DB against attacks from the
```
front, and the effect will last for 2 minutes (1 min-
```
```
ute/level based on a 2nd level spell). If a single dose
```
of the potion was created, it would take 1+3+2 =
6 days, with a material cost of 3sp per day totaling
18sp and a market price of 30sp. However, batch
production will reduce this cost. For a batch of five
such potions, “Work Mundane Liquid” and “Make
```
Potion III” must be cast on every item (a total of 20
```
```
PP), plus one single casting of “Shield” (an addi-
```
```
tional 2 PP), so 22 PP of spells must be cast every
```
day for the same 6 days as for a single dose. The
material cost and market price for the batch are the
```
same as for the single dose (material cost of 18sp
```
```
total, market price of 30sp total), but this permits
```
the individual potions to be sold for one fifth the
price, or 6sp apiece. The alchemist could produce
larger or smaller batches, limited by their supply
of PP. Note that batches of more advanced potions
are likely to exceed the 40 PP per day threshold and
therefore incur a slightly higher market price.
```
Example: For a Psychographer to create a Shield
```
potion, the alchemist must cast Work Mundane Liq-
```
uid (level 1), Enchanting Elixir I (level 1), Emplace
```
```
Spell III (level 5), either a Shield spell (level 3) or
```
an additional casting of Emplace Spell III, and if
the Psychographer does not know the Shield spell
themselves, once at the initiation of enchantment
they must cast a Template Link spell to someone
who does. After drinking the potion, for the next
15 minutes the drinker will gain the ability to cast
```
the Shield spell (only if they have Mentalism PP,
```
though this can be worked around as with perma-
```
nent items). A single dose would take 10 days to
```
produce, with a material cost of 5sp per day totaling
50sp, and a market price of 70sp. Batch production
reduces the market price to 14sp. This approach to
creating potions is valuable primarily when the user
will want to cast the spell multiple times within the
duration, otherwise it is generally less efficient and
slower-acting than the Adept's potions.
```
Example: A Psychographer might create an elixir
```
which grants reasonable competency with a melee
weapon. With Work Mundane Liquid, Enchanting
```
Elixir I, Physical Skill II (level 11), and a Template
```
Link to someone with 20 ranks in Melee Weap-
```
ons: Blade (+80 skill bonus), they could produce
```
a potion that grants the imbiber a +80 x 40% =
+32 skill bonus when fighting with a weapon in the
Blades category. While this isn't of much use to a
skilled warrior, it could be a considerable aid to a
noble's child or other untrained individual. Such a
potion would take 13 days to create, with a material
cost of 143sp and a market price of 165sp. Batch
production will require the expenditure of 65 PP/
day, resulting in a market price of 36.4sp for the
batch-produced potion.
132
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
Power Enhancers
```
(Adept, Sanctifier)
```
The most common types of power enhancers are Spell
```
Adders and PP Multipliers. (PP Adders are created as
```
bonus items, using the General or Sacred Skill spells
```
to grant a skill bonus to Power Development.) Sanc-
```
tifiers may create Spell Adders, while Adept options
include Spell Adders, PP Multipliers, Casting Focuses
```
(which grant a bonus to spell casting rolls), and Ele-
```
```
mental Power items (which grant a bonus to ball and
```
```
cone spells).
```
To create a power enhancer, the following spells must
be cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3b) or
```
Consecration.
```
• An appropriate power enhancing spell (from the
```
Adept base list Enchantment Mastery or the
```
Sanctifier base list Sacred Purpose).
```
A Standard Power Enhancer made by an Adept works
for either a single realm or for a single Hybrid profes-
```
sion; halve the spell (half working days and half PP) if
```
it will work only for a single non-Hybrid profession,
double it for items that work for two realms, and triple
it for items that work for all realms. A Spell Adder
made by a Sanctifier will work for any spell appropri-
```
ate to the caster's deity (e.g., fire spells), regardless of
```
realm.
These items must normally be held in hand to receive
```
their benefits (e.g., a wand), but the spell may be dou-
```
bled if the benefit is to be received when it is merely
worn. Holding a power enhancer is not considered to
```
impair spellcasting; you are still considered to have
```
your hand free for purposes of the spell-casting modi-
fiers on Spell Law’s Table 4-2.
```
Rune Paper (Adept, Sanctifier)
```
Rune Paper is a magical paper, parchment, vellum or
other writing material that has been specially prepared
to hold spells, which non-alchemist spell casters can
inscribe using spells from the Rune Mastery spell list.
As noted in Section 2.7, multiple sheets of Rune Paper
may be bound together in a volume called a “spell-
book”, allowing for easier carrying and quicker Rune
location than a collection of individual sheets.
To create a Rune Paper, the following spells must be
cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (Adept: Work Mundane
```
Paper, level 1, or Work Mundane Leather, level
```
3, for parchment)
```
```
• Make Rune Paper (the 5th level spell from either
```
the Adept base list Organic Skills or the Sanctifi-
```
er base list Sacred Runes).
```
```
Example: An adept alchemist wishes to create Rune
```
Paper. This enchantment simply requires a daily
level 1 “Work Mundane Paper” spell and a level 5
“Make Rune Paper” spell to create the Rune Paper
itself. If a single sheet was to be created, it would
take 6 days, with a material cost of 30sp, and a
market price of 42sp. However, batch production
will reduce this cost to 8.4sp.
Table 5-3f: Liquid, Gas, and Poison Work Spells
Material Adept Psychographer
```
Mundane Liquid Work Mundane Liquid (1) Work Mundane Liquid (1)
```
```
Magical Liquid Work Magical Liquid (11) n/a
```
```
Mundane Gas Work Mundane Gas (5) n/a
```
```
Magical Gas Work Magical Gas (18) n/a
```
```
Poison (up to 5th level) Work Poison I (4) n/a
```
```
Poison (up to 10th level) Work Poison II (10) n/a
```
```
Poison (up to 15th level) Work Poison III (16) n/a
```
```
Poison (above 15th level) Work Poison True (25) n/a
```
Table 5-3g: Potions
```
Adept Spell (Level) Psychographer Spell (Level)
```
```
Spells up to 3rd level Make Potion III (3) Duration of 15 min Enchanting Elixir I (1)
```
```
Spells up to 6th level Make Potion VI (9) Duration of 1 hour Enchanting Elixir II (3)
```
```
Spells up to 10th level Make Potion X (15) Duration of 1 day Enchanting Elixir III (9)
```
```
Spells up to 20th level Make Potion XX (20)
```
```
Spells up to any level Make Potion True (25)
```
133
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
```
Sacred Spell Items (Sanctifier)
```
These items have a single spell embedded within
them, usable once per day. They are used much like
Daily items, although they lack the flexibility of the
Daily enchantment. In general they are easier and less
expensive to create, but both the item and the spell
must be closely related to the religion of the Sanctifier
and the enchantment should have a purpose aligned
with that of the faith.
To create a Sacred Spell item, the following spells
must be cast every day:
```
• Consecration (the 2nd level spell from the Sanctifi-
```
```
er base list Sacred Purpose).
```
```
• An appropriate Sacred Spell spell (from the Sancti-
```
```
fier base list Divine Touch).
```
• The spell to be embedded.
```
Example: A Sanctifier wishes to create a helm which
```
will cast True Aura once per day in order to pro-
tect warriors of the faith. The spells required are
```
“Consecration” (2nd level), “Sacred Spell VI”
```
```
(15th level), and “True Aura” (level 6 from the open
```
```
Channeling spell list Light’s Way). Once created, it
```
will allow the wearer to create an overwhelmingly
bright and powerful aura once per day, subtracting
15 from any attacks against them. The enchantment
will require 2+15+6 = 23 days, with a material cost
of 15sp per day totaling 345sp, and a market price
of 406sp including 15sp for a heavy helm. Howev-
er, the Sanctifier is unlikely to sell such an item to
anyone not of their faith.
```
Single Use Items (Adept)
```
An Alchemist may decide, for whatever reason, to cre-
ate an object, such as a weapon, ring or wand, that has
one or more spells embedded in it, but once the spell is
```
used, it is gone forever. This tends to be quicker (and
```
```
thus cheaper) than creating a more permanent item,
```
and it can also benefit from batch production. The
spell may require attunement to activate, or may be
```
triggered by a pre-defined condition (e.g., a single use
```
arrow may trigger its spell when it inflicts a critical.
Single use items may be created with such triggers for
```
no additional cost (i.e., without the need for Triggered
```
```
Item).
```
To create a single use item, the following spells must
be cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3b).
```
```
• Appropriate Embed spell(s).
```
```
• The spell(s) to be embedded.
```
```
Example: An Adept wishes to create an emergen-
```
cy item that will allow a one-off use of the level 6
“Invisibility II” spell. This item is to be a leather
headband, and so requires a daily casting of the
level 3 “Work Mundane Leather” spell and the level
15 “Embed VII” spell. It will also require the level
6 invisibility spell to be cast each day. Once used,
the user will become invisible for up to 24 hours
as per the spell. The headband will then become a
simple leather headband. If a single headband was
to be created, it would take 3+15+6 = 24 days, with
a material cost of 15sp per day totaling 360sp and
a market price of 408sp. However, batch production
```
will reduce this cost (compare the example under
```
```
Batch Production).
```
```
Skill Items (Psychographer)
```
While Adepts and Sanctifiers create items of magical
quality, Psychographers use their Skill Impressions
base list to create items which contain the knowledge
of how they should be wielded. Rather than granting
bonuses to the applicable skill, the knowledge in such
an item replaces the user’s skill entirely. This knowl-
edge must be copied from a person who serves as the
template for the enchantment, either the alchemist
themself or a person who is present during the enchant-
```
ing process (within 100'). If the skill requires special-
```
ization, the bonus will be for a specific specialization.
When using the item, the wielder is treated as if they
had the higher of the item’s skill bonus or their own
```
skill bonus (even if they do not have the skill at all).
```
They still apply their own stat bonus as well as any
```
professional and knack bonuses (unless those are
```
also embedded via the Professional Bonus and Knack
```
spells). They do not receive the benefit of the item’s
```
```
skill ranks (e.g., for passive dodge or block or for Am-
```
```
bush) unless Rank Impression is also used to enchant
```
the item.
To create a Skill item, the following spells must be
cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3b).
```
• The Skill, Knack, Professional Bonus and/or other
spells from the Skill Impressions base list. The
selected spell will determine how much of the
template’s skill will be stored in the item.
```
Template Link (the 1st level spell from the Power Im-
```
```
pressions base list) must be cast once at the beginning
```
of the process if the knowledge will be copied from
someone other than the alchemist.
134
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
```
Example: A Psychographer wishes to create a spear
```
which may be wielded by anyone. They obtain the
services of a skilled fighter with a skill rank bonus
```
of +80 (not including the fighter’s stat bonus or pro-
```
```
fessional bonus). They enchant the weapon with the
```
level 8 “Work Common Metals” spell and choose
the 17th level “Physical Skill III,” each of these
spells being cast each day during its creation, and
will also need a single casting of “Template Link”
```
(level 1) to benefit from the fighter’s knowledge. The
```
item will take 25 days to create, with a material cost
of 17sp per day totaling 425sp, and a market price
```
of 475sp (ignoring the 9bp cost of a spear, since it is
```
```
less than 1sp).
```
```
The item will grant a skill bonus of +48 (60% of the
```
```
fighter’s skill bonus) and also benefit from the wield-
```
```
er’s stat bonus (if any). It will not be of great use to
```
```
other skilled combatants (since it replaces rather
```
```
than augmenting their skill) but may be of consider-
```
able value to those with little or no martial training.
An alternative approach for a Psychographer is to
create an item which grants the Prodigy talent in a de-
```
sired skill. A Talent Embedding spell (from the Power
```
```
Impressions base list) replaces the Skill spell. If the
```
wielder already has a Prodigy talent for the same skill,
they will receive only the higher bonus of the two, but
this does permit a bonus to their existing skill.
```
Example: A Psychographer wishes to create a spear
```
which improves the wielder’s ability. They obtain
the services of a skilled fighter with Prodigy III in
Melee Weapons. They enchant the weapon with the
level 8 “Work Common Metals” plus level 19 “Tal-
```
ent Embedding IV” (which permits a talent up to 20
```
```
DP to be embedded), plus one casting of “Template
```
Link.” The item will take 27 days to create, with a
material cost of 19sp per day totaling 513sp, and a
market price of 567sp.
The item will grant the wielder the Prodigy III talent
in Melee. If the wielder does not already have Prodigy
in Melee, their skill gains a +15 bonus. If they already
have Prodigy in Melee, only the greater of the two
bonuses applies.
```
Weapons (Adept, Sanctifier)
```
Ever-popular, enchanted weapons may be crafted by
both Adepts and Sanctifiers. Weapons and missiles
```
(e.g., arrows) enchanted with a Sacred Weapon spell
```
receive an OB bonus, while those enchanted with a
Weapon spell may receive either a general OB bonus
or an OB bonus which applies against a specific Type
```
of foe (e.g., reptiles, demons). When creating a bonus
```
against a certain set of creatures, the time required for
the Weapon spell is halved if it only applies against a
```
specific Variety (e.g., alligators, corvox), or doubled
```
```
if it applies against an entire Category (e.g., animals,
```
```
extra planar creatures). (See Creature Law for details
```
about the Variety, Type, and Category of each crea-
```
ture.) A weapon may have both a general bonus and
```
one or more bonuses against different types of foes,
and receives both the general and specific bonuses
when used against the specified type of foe.
```
Ranged weapons which launch missiles (e.g., bows,
```
```
crossbows, slings) enchanted with a Range Improve-
```
ment or Sacred Weapon spell receive a bonus to their
base range increment. Such weapons are not eligible
to be enchanted to grant OB bonuses.
To create a magic weapon, the following spells must
be cast every day:
```
• An appropriate Work spell (see Table 5-3b for
```
```
Adepts; Sanctifiers use Consecration, the 2nd
```
```
level spell from the Sacred Purpose base list).
```
```
• The Bonus Effect spell(s) (Adepts use spells from
```
```
their Weapon Enchantment base list; Sanctifiers
```
```
use their Divine Touch base list).
```
```
Example: An Adept wants to create a +10 magical
```
sword. This requires a daily casting of the level 5
“Work Lesser Metals” spell and the level 8 “Weap-
on II” spell. If the Adept wanted to enhance the
```
weapon with an additional Fire critical (two de-
```
```
grees of severity less than the primary critical) and
```
an extra +10 against Orcs, the level 7 “Additional
```
Crit I” spell and lvl 8 “Weapon II” spell (again)
```
would also need to be cast daily. The enhanced
weapon would take 5+8+7+8 = 28 days to create,
with a material cost of 8sp per day totaling 224sp.
The market price will be 280sp, plus the base price
of a broadsword for 10sp, for a total of 290sp.
Other enchantments commonly placed on weapons
```
(on the Weapon Enchantment base list) include Ad-
```
```
ditional Crit (the weapon deals an additional critical
```
of a specified type, e.g., the weapon is flaming or
```
freezing), Slaying (the weapon receives a bonus on
```
```
critical rolls usually against a particular type of foe),
```
```
and Increased Size (the weapon strikes as if it were
```
```
larger). These spells may be used on both melee and
```
ranged weapons.135
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
Slaying effects normally apply to a specific Variety
```
of creature (e.g., bears), a single profession, or Evil
```
spellcasters.
Other Items
The list of item types given here is far from exhaus-
tive. Psychographers and Sanctifiers can even enchant
locations, and Sanctifiers can use tattoos to enchant a
person's skin. In general, Adepts and Psychographers
will create other types of items by casting an appro-
priate Work spell followed by whatever combination
of other spells are desired. Sanctifiers usually begin
with a Consecration spell. Consult the spell lists for a
complete list of options.
aRtifact intelligence
There are two possible ways to make an artifact intel-
ligent. A new mind may be created essentially from
nothing, or an existing intelligence may be embedded
in the item.
New Intelligence
The first option is to create an artificial intelligence
```
using the Artificial Intelligence spell (Mechanical
```
```
Mastery), Blessed Thoughts spell (Sacred Purpose) or
```
```
Shadow Soul spell (Personal Empowerment), creating
```
a truly artificial intelligence that is capable of rational
thought.
When intelligence is created, the newly created intelli-
gence will have six mental stat bonuses. If the Shadow
Soul spell is used, the stat bonus values are explained
in the specific spell. For the other two spells, the table
below is used to generate the bonuses. These spells
can create various grades of intelligence, and the ap-
propriate column should be used. A d100 should be
rolled for each of the six stats: Em, In, Me, Pr, Re and
SD and the bonus determined from the table.
Once all of the six individual stat bonuses have been
generated, they should be totalled. This total is the
Will characteristic of the item.
The intelligent item has a limited awareness of its sur-
roundings, but is able to sense minds within a radius
equal to its level in feet. When it has sensed a mind,
it can read the aura of that being and determine race,
sex, profession, realm and a general impression of
personality. If someone is in direct contact with the
item or attuned to it, the item can initiate a Will con-
```
test to try to dominate that person (see Intelligence in
```
```
Section 3.3 for details). The item can also communi-
```
```
cate with a being within range by general empathy;
```
this communication consists of general feelings rath-
er than words. If the item is attuned to someone, the
item can also sense the world through the senses of its
wielder. There are also various spells which will allow
improved communication.
An artificially intelligent item may be made with
knowledge up to one rank in spell lists per level of the
```
item’s creator (if Spellcaster was used in the enchant-
```
```
ment) and up to two ranks in other skills per level of
```
the item’s creator, where the creator is considered to be
the individual who casts the spell granting intelligence.
All spells and skill ranks are limited to those known
```
by those who participate in enchanting the item (cast
```
```
at least one of the Alchemical spells involved). This
```
is further limited by the item’s capacity for learning
as shown in Table 5-3i, and the creator may choose
to leave some ranks open for future adaptation in any
case. A casting of Additional Skills will increase the
maximum number of skills or the maximum number
of ranks per level for one skill by one intelligence
```
category (e.g., from medium intelligence to high in-
```
```
telligence).
```
136
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
If the item has capacity to learn additional skills or
spell lists, teaching it normally requires one month of
daily one-hour training sessions by an attuned wielder
```
in a skill or spell list they know (and limited by their
```
```
number of ranks).
```
Once chosen, the final skill bonus can be calculated, as
for a character, using the stat bonuses generated above.
If the item is to have further enchantments such as
bonuses, daily powers, etc., these must be cast at the
```
same time as the artificial intelligence spell(s) and the
```
AI spells will also add towards the time and material
costs totals.
Finally, there is the matter of the item’s purpose, ethics,
and personality. These do not require further spells,
but are a necessary consequence of intelligence. If
these aspects are not filled in during creation, they will
emerge through early experiences in the item’s career.
Items created with Blessed Thoughts will have their
outlooks heavily influenced by the current orthodoxy
of the religion of the creating Sanctifier, although
some variation may arise, especially if the Sanctifier
is out of the mainstream. In any case, the particular
purpose of the item may be set. Two swords of the
same faith will be quite different to wield if one was
made to protect pilgrims, while the other was made to
punish heresy. An Adept using Artificial Intelligence
has a freer hand, though he will likely want to keep the
item’s ethical outlook close to his own. If an Alchemist
gets particularly ambitious in the detail he wishes to
give an item’s personality, the GM may call for a Spell
Mastery roll in order to get it exactly right, but nor-
mally these things will go as planned, barring a spell
failure, which can make things go very differently.
Possible purposes include: defend the helpless, uphold
```
justice (which could mean a lot of different things),
```
promote mercy, slay the enemies of a given religion,
slay the enemies of a given nation, slay the enemies
of a given people, slay the enemies of a given family,
eliminate Evil spellcasters, eliminate created entities
```
(golems & constructs), eliminate shapechangers,
```
eliminate the undead, eliminate dragonkind, eliminate
elves, commit general mayhem, support the king of a
given nation, obtain a skilled user, serve a politically
powerful individual, obtain wealth, obtain fame/glory,
learn magical secrets, eliminate rival items, torment its
wielder, death to all tyrants, defy authority, establish
a new kingdom, punish liars, pranks and merriment,
abolish slavery, destroy that demon liquor, explore
new lands, promote honest business, kill babies, start
wars, prevent wars, and so on.
```
Example: An Adept wishes to create an intelligent
```
magical staff. The base spell required is Artificial
```
Intelligence III (level 9) which will create the intel-
```
ligence itself. To grant it telepathic communication,
a double casting of Improved Communication is
```
used (effective level 8). Sensory Enhancement (level
```
```
5) is added to allow the staff to see. Lastly, the staff
```
should be able to cast a few spells to help its owner,
```
so Spellcaster is also needed (level 8). As a magic
```
item, this spell will also require the level 4 Work
Mundane Wood to actually create the magical item.
This is 34 levels of spells, so it will take 34 days and
cost 306sp to create, or 370sp to purchase.

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
The staff will have Medium grade intelligence,
and so rolls are made to generate the mental stat
```
bonuses:
```
• Em – roll 95 – +3 bonus
• In – roll 69 – +1 bonus
• Me – roll 05 – -3 bonus
• Pr – roll 81 – +2 bonus
• Re – roll 50 – +0 bonus
• SD – roll 81 – +2 bonus
The sum of these bonuses is +5, which is the Will of
the staff.
The staff will also know up to four Spell lists. It will
```
have 9 ranks in Power Development (one per level),
```
giving it a total of 51 PP.
The adept may well add an OB bonus, a daily pow-
er, charged powers, etc. at the time of enchantment,
but that is another matter.
The Adept would also have to determine the role of
the staff, its personality, etc.
Embedded Entities
The process described above for creating artificial
intelligences creates an entirely new rational being to
populate the item. The second method of creating an
intelligent magical item is rather different. Through the
use of Bind [Entity] spells, an Alchemist can trap a will-
ing being within the item. It is important that the being
be willing, otherwise the spells will fail. Coercion or
magical charm may be used to make the entity willing,
or the being may consent of their own choice, but they
must be willing for the duration of the creation process.
So why would an entity consent to such a thing? Leav-
ing aside the very real possibility of magical compul-
sion, there are many possibilities. The alternative may
simply be more uninviting: a dying man or a defeated
being facing destruction may prefer preservation within
an item. Some might agree out of a sense of curiosity.
For an immortal being, spending some span of time as
a sword or shield or key might offer some amusement.
Some might be willing to strike a bargain if the incen-
```
tive is great enough; the less-intelligent entities may
```
even strike ill-considered bargains. Others might agree
as part of existing service, whether ultimately motivated
by loyalty, fear of a superior, or a sense of duty. While
it is not so easy to convince a powerful demon to enter
an item willingly, one is much more likely to offer one
of its minions for such a service. Members of services
like an order of Paladins or a royal guard might have
a tradition of carrying on after death by becoming the
gear of their replacements. Some might like the power
of the body provided by the item, especially if it is a
self-moving item like a construct or golem. In other cas-
es, the purpose of the item and the entity may be one.
If a being truly hates elves, becoming the motivating
spirit of a powerful Elf-slaying sword may be the most
effective method of acting on that hatred. Occasionally,
an entity will lose a wager and enter an item as a forfeit.
Tales tell of entities variously tricked into service, but
the practicality of such an approach will depend on the
setting and on the type of entity involved.
```
Having obtained a (technically) willing entity, a suit-
```
able Bind [Entity] spell is used to actually embed the
entity’s spirit within the item. These spells are found
on the Elemental Binding, Spirit Binding and Demonic
Binding lists.
In the case of a disembodied spirit, a demon, or an ele-
mental, the whole entity is drawn into the item. When a
spirit is taken from a living entity or corporeal undead
entity, a body is left behind, and it will be subject to nor-
mal forces of degeneration. An old undead body may
turn to dust within seconds of losing its animating spirit.
```
A dying body will die on schedule; other living bodies
```
are comatose, as if subjected to Absolution. If the body
is preserved, the spirit may be able to return to it, if it
is ever released from the item in which it is embedded.
Entities cannot be embedded in items that are already
enchanted, though they may be added to a golem or
construct once the body is completed and before the en-
chantment creating it is finished. If the item is reworked,
the embedding spell must be cast as one of the enchant-
```
ment spells, and the entity will remain in the item (bar-
```
```
ring catastrophic spell failure). The exception is that a
```
Manifest spell may be used after the initial creation of
the item, along with a suitable Work or Consecration
spell, to make more of the entity’s abilities accessible
through the item without the need for redoing the rest of
the enchantment.
The result of a successful embedding, at the conclusion
of the enchantment process, is that the item is now mag-
ical, as well as being possessed of intelligence and will.
It has all of the stat bonuses of the embedded entity for
Empathy, Intelligence, Memory, Presence, Reasoning,
```
and Self Discipline (Agility, Constitution, Quickness,
```
```
and Strength do not normally apply to items; even in
```
the case of golems and constructs, these are properties
```
of the artificial body, not the embedded spirit). Will is
```
based on these stat bonuses, although demonic entities
are harder to control and receive a +10 bonus to Will
in addition to whatever modification applies from their
stats.
The newly-embedded entity also has access to any Lore
skills it possesses and the lore aspects of its other skills.
Practical application of its skills requires a Manifest
Skill enchantment. Other abilities based on the embed-
ded entity will also require the use of spells from the
```
appropriate list (Demonic Binding, Elemental Binding,
```
```
or Spirit Binding).138
```
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
```
Example: A Psychographer is creating a dagger with
```
malicious intent, and has arranged with a murderer
```
(and his executioner) to bind the murderer’s spirit
```
into the blade. The preparation progress will finish
at the time the murderer is hung, capturing his spirit
```
using the Bind Rational Spirit spell (level 10). The
```
level 12 spell Manifest Talent III will be cast to al-
low the wielder to use the “Pressing the Advantage
II” talent, and the level 14 spell Manifest Stat will
allow the wielder to use the +12 strength bonus of
the murderer. Lastly, the dagger itself is made from
```
high steel (+10) and so the Work Mundane Metals
```
```
II spell (level 12) is needed.
```
This totals 48 levels of spells to be cast each day for
```
38 days (reduced due to the material), and will cost
```
532 sp in reagents. If the item is sold, the market
```
price would add 3sp per day (due to a PP cost of
```
```
48/day) plus 103sp for the high steel dagger, for a
```
total of 749sp.
loRd ReseaRch
```
The Lord Research spell (level 25 on the Symbolic
```
```
Ways list or Rune Mastery list) is a method by which
```
Channeling and Essence spell-casters who are not
Alchemists can create magical items. This process
is quite different from the methods of the Alchemist
and is far less controlled. The creator can attempt to
influence the nature of the results, but they are largely
random, at least from the point-of-view of the charac-
ter. Results are determined by the GM, who may use a
random method, or simply design an item that will be
interesting in itself, present new plot opportunities, or
meet a coming need of the creator and their associates.
The last is particularly appropriate for Channeling
Lord Research, where the caster’s deity may intervene
in the process to ensure his or her servant has some
necessary power.
To power this form of enchantment, the natural es-
sence bound into gem stones must be released and
utilized. An Essence user casting this spell will feed
```
the gems into a special forge (treat this as a type of
```
```
workshop with a base cost of 800sp), and a Channel-
```
ing user will sacrifice the gems to their God. Either
way, the gems are destroyed. This process requires
that 250sp worth of gems be expended every day, and
the spell itself cast three times each day. The process
ends either when the caster wishes, or when a full day
passes without the expenditure of gems or the casting
of spells. This process cannot benefit from assistants.
During the process, the character must supply up to
six different items to receive the enchantment. These
items must be on the forge or altar for the whole
process, and cannot be magical. They must also all
be different. A two handed sword and a broadsword
would count as two of the required items, whilst two
broadswords would not, even if they are crafted from
different materials. If there are less than six qualifying
items, other nearby items will occupy the required
slots. These could be a beer mug, doormat or other
mundane item or could even be an item of clothing
worn by the spell caster. The caster will never know
which mundane items are part of the roster in this in-
stance.
Table 5-3j: Lord Research Spell Mastery
Total Result
<1 Absolute Failure: The item enchanted will not be
from the six offered, but will be some other nearby
unsuitable and inconvenient item.
1-75 Failure: A nearby item acquires the enchantment.
76-100 Partial Success: As Success.
```
101-175 Success: One of the offered items (selected random-
```
```
ly) acquires the enchantment.
```
176+ Absolute Success: The spell caster can choose which
of the offered items receives the enchantment.139
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Treasure Law
At the end of the process, the spellcaster must make a
Spell Mastery roll for his Lord Research spell. If the
number of days spent on this process is not divisible
by 5, any “spare” days will grant a +10 bonus to this
Spell Mastery roll.
Once the item to be enchanted has been determined,
the actual powers should be determined. The item will
have one magical ability per full five days of the en-
chantment process.
The best way to determine these powers is for the GM
to design the item to fit the campaign. As a rule, the
powers will fit the item. Thus a weapon will have bo-
nuses to OB, extra criticals or damage or even protec-
tive abilities. Shields and Armor will increase DB and
have other protective powers. Rings and other mis-
cellaneous items will have embedded spells and daily
powers. If the spellcaster is a channeller, the patron
deity will also influence the abilities. A god of winter
might impart an extra cold crit to a weapon, protection
vs cold to a shield etc. The materials or design of the
item may also influence the outcome. It should be not-
ed that the powers enchanted by Lord Research can be
those available to alchemists, or could be completely
new and unique.
If the GM wishes inspiration, they may roll on the
random power table above to determine the general
form of each power. The results should be consid-
ered suggestions and not binding. A GM could also
create their own table specific for their campaign. It
is recommended that the GM add some unusual twist
to the item, even if it is purely in terms of superficial
properties with no effects in terms of game mechanics
```
(e.g., a Spell Adder chimes like a bell whenever it is
```
```
used to cast a spell).
```
```
Example: A Ranger has offered a shield, a pair of
```
boots, an arming sword, a cloak, a longbow and a
helm as candidates for enchantment. With a good
Spell Mastery roll, he is able to select the shield to
be enchanted. He has been enchanting for 10 days
and offered 2,500sp in gems so the item will have
two enchantments. The GM rolls for two abilities,
which come up as DB bonus and Daily spell power,
then designs the following item:
Winged Shield: This is a +15 oval shield with
a pair of white wings on a blue field. When the
```
shield’s Fly II (cast at 40th level) is activated, which
```
can be done twice a day, the raised wings move to
the sides of the shield and flap. The spell can only
target the shield’s bearer and if the shield is some-
how lost mid-flight, the spell will be broken. Howev-
er, the weight allowance of the spell is 1500 pounds,
regardless of the weight of the shield-bearer. This is
a hickory normal shield bound with high steel.
otheR oRigins
In addition to the spell-based item creation methods
specified above, there are a number of other sources of
magic items that may replace or supplement the work
of Alchemists in some campaigns. These generally
exist quite outside of the standard item creation sys-
tem. It is for the GM to decide which, if any, of these
methods will be used in his campaign setting. In some
worlds, one or more of these options may replace the
Alchemists’ arts completely.

Roll

```
(specialized)
```

```
(specialized)
```

ability
4 Negative trait
```
(and roll again)
```

```
(and roll again)
```

```
(and roll again)
```

power

power
Daily spell power

9 Stat bonus Stat bonus Stat bonus

ability

```
(e.g., wandlike)
```
